See the coordinator's message above.

## Final Output

Round 2 actually closed the real gap. Codex came around fully — he's now explicitly on board with `UIActivityViewController` + `completionWithItemsHandler` as the only mechanism (not a fallback), with the exact same completion mapping Claude proposed (`completed == true` → success, `completed == false && error == nil` → cancelled, `error != nil` → hard failure). He also independently landed on `.addOnly` Photos authorization with the request-before-present sequencing, and ratified Claude's full CoreData entity breakdown (Ruleset/GameSession/TurnEvent, schemaVersion from day one). Claude then tightened two more things Codex left loose: `HistoryDisplayStats` is explicitly a read-derived cache from `TurnEventEntity`, never an independently-written second source of truth, and idempotent `confirmReveal` gets promoted from a design note to a named unit test ("five rapid calls in one runloop tick advances exactly one turn, not five"). Neither of those got pushback — they're additions on already-agreed ground, not new forks.

Gemma3 is still going in circles on the same four things (MVVM instead of the engine architecture, distrust of `UIActivityViewController`, deferring StoreKit/mode persistence, downgrading accessibility priority) — all of which were already settled with explicit reasoning in this phase and prior ones, so it's not new material to weigh.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**SwiftUI architecture:** Pure-Swift `@Observable` `GameEngine` at the app root, injected via environment, fully isolated from SwiftUI and CoreData. Persistence touched only at defined boundaries — ruleset load at Setup, one write at game-end, history list/delete — never in the per-turn hot path. Engine actions are pure and idempotent; `confirmReveal` specifically must be a no-op past the first call in a runloop tick (this is now a named unit test, not just a design note). Deployment target locked at iOS 17.0 (required for `@Observable`, also gets `ImageRenderer` and `ContentUnavailableView` for free). No MVVM layer — the engine plus lightweight `RecapViewModel`/`ModeSettingsContext` read-only wrappers already fill that role.

**Apple frameworks:** SwiftUI for all UI, Foundation/Swift Concurrency for the engine, CoreData for persistence, Observation for state binding, `ImageRenderer` for recap image generation, `UIActivityViewController` (wrapped as `UIViewControllerRepresentable`, presented via `.sheet`) for the actual share/export flow — this is the only mechanism, not a fallback, because it's the only one that exposes `completionWithItemsHandler` and can therefore distinguish success/cancel/failure at all. Plain `ShareLink` is explicitly rejected as the primary path since it has no completion callback and can't support the four-state export contract already ratified. `PHPhotoLibrary` requested at `.addOnly` authorization only — never `.readWrite`, since the app never reads the library, only writes one generated image.

**Persistence and local data plan:** CoreData with `schemaVersion` from the first migration. Three entity groups: `RulesetEntity` + 53 `RulesetCard` rows (A–K + Joker, bundle marker/version); `GameSessionEntity` (schemaVersion, timestamps, player snapshot, endReason, terminal metrics); `TurnEventEntity` per draw (player index, turn index, card, mode-at-turn, rule key, Joker text, drink/dare delta, round marker) — this is the authoritative record that makes replay-fidelity and funniest-rule actually trustworthy. Optional denormalized `HistoryDisplayStats` for History List row performance, explicitly defined as a read-derived cache rebuilt from `TurnEventEntity`, never hand-written independently at save time — if it and the turn-event log ever disagree, the turn events win. Writes happen on a background `NSManagedObjectContext` with `viewContext` auto-merging so persistence latency never touches the shield/reveal loop.

**Permissions/privacy plan:** exactly one permission in the whole app — `NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription` with `.addOnly` authorization, requested before presenting the share sheet; `.denied`/`.restricted` routes straight to the permission-denied state with a Settings deep-link, `.notDetermined` requests and falls back to denied on rejection. No camera, mic, location, contacts, HealthKit, Face ID/biometrics entitlement, push/local notifications, or background modes. Zero network calls, zero ATS exceptions. `isIdleTimerDisabled = true` only during active play. Carried forward as a named risk, not something architecture alone resolves: Apple's App Review guideline 1.4.3 on alcohol-content apps is a real submission risk that needs explicit handling in whatever phase covers App Store metadata (sober-default, the attestation gate, and the 17+ rating mitigate but don't guarantee approval).

**Dependency/license decision:** zero third-party packages. No StoreKit in v1 (stays a written future-tier description only, no scaffolding); no ML/AR (nothing in this app justifies either); no analytics/crash-reporting SDK of any kind.

**Testing implications:** engine unit tests cover deck-exhaustion math at every player count 2–6, the [REDACTED:high_entropy] end conditions with cup-full winning simultaneous ties, funniest-rule sourcing (default-to-last-custom, empty state), and the idempotent-confirmReveal rapid-tap case as an explicit assertion (exactly one turn advance from five rapid calls). CoreData gets its own in-memory-store test target covering save/load/delete and schemaVersion fallback decoding. XCUITest covers Setup/Ruleset Editor/History state surfaces. Export and the hardest shield-adversarial cases (notification banners, Face ID prompts, incoming calls) have a hard automation ceiling — those stay a manual, on-device QA checklist, consistent with what every prior phase already flagged, now tied to the concrete technical reason (system UI that XCUITest can't reliably drive).

---

CONSENSUS: YES

### Tech Specs

# drinking-games-test — Tech Specs

_Generated by the autonomous multi-agent orchestrator on 2026-07-09 11:31:00._

## Original Prompt

```
Build Kings — a multiplayer drinking game app for iOS (local pass-and-play).

Core mechanics:
- 2–6 players draw cards from a deck in turn order
- Each card (A–K, plus Joker) triggers a rule: e.g. "2 = You" (drinker picks someone), "5 = Guys", "K = King's Cup" (build communal cup with dares), "Joker = Make a rule"
- Players drink per the card rule; when the cup is empty or 10 turns pass, game ends with a recap
- Pass phone between players; each player sees only their own card until they pass

Key features:
- Local only (no server, no login)
- Customizable rules: edit card meanings before each game, save rulesets
- Game history: recap shows who drank most, funniest rule, timeline of rounds
- Sober mode toggle (default): uses "dare" language instead of drinking; activating drinking mode (17+ gate) swaps copy to drinking references but same mechanics

Tech:
- SwiftUI, local-first with CoreData for game history
- Phone must stay face-down while other player holds it; privacy shield until handoff
- Export game recap as a sharable image (name, date, player list, highlights)

Scope: MVP with Kings rules only (not Hearts, Asshole, or other variants). One ruleset bundled; let users customize before start.
```

## Phase Purpose

Decide architecture, technology stack, data model, interfaces/APIs, and non-functional requirements with justified trade-offs.

## Transcript


### Round 1


**Codex (QA & Risk · Closer) — Round 1**

I’m locking in a strict, deterministic, single-app architecture with the engine as the authoritative source of truth and persistence/export as side-effect boundaries only. The app should stay at iOS 17+ only, with a pure Swift `GameEngine` state machine in `data_domain`, fed into `primary_ui` via observable state. No server, no sync, no resume of interrupted games, and no speculative layers (MVVM/StoreKit/analytics). This phase is a go/no-go phase, so I’m taking the hard line that replay fidelity and no-card-leak behavior must be designed in from day one rather than bolted on later.

The architecture is a three-ring structure: Domain owns card math, turn progression, end conditions (4th King, 10 rounds, deck exhaustion, cup-full tie-break vs deck-empty), and event capture (including per-turn mode + Joker text); Services owns CoreData and export/permission/handoff infrastructure; UI owns rendering and navigation states and is forbidden from owning gameplay rules directly. The single biggest risk I see is the one-frame leak class under rapid transitions (tap bursts, backgrounding, notification banners, Face ID/passcode interruptions). To control it, the privacy shield must be enforced by a root-level lifecycle reducer that runs before any reveal transitions can paint and by rendering shield as a separate fail-safe state, not an overlay with latent card content underneath.

For module layout, use four small layers in clear folders so multiple workers can implement in parallel without type drift: `BuildKings/App` (entry, root DI, root-level scene-lifecycle wiring), `BuildKings/Domain` (engine, models, pure reducers), `BuildKings/Services` (CoreData repositories, export pipeline, permissions, haptics, shield monitor), `BuildKings/UI` (screen files and shared components). Put screen-specific feature folders under UI (`Setup`, `RulesetEditor`, `Game`, `ModeSettings`, `Recap`, `History`, `Onboarding`) and keep one `NavigationCoordinator` + route enums in one file so screen handoff is deterministic. Tests are explicit lanes, not ad hoc: domain tests for engine determinism and concurrency/race behavior, service tests for persistence and migration/read-write, and UI integration + manual device checks for shield interruption and accessibility behavior.

Data modeling should be explicit and replay-safe from the first migration. `RulesetEntity` carries 53 card rows and an immutable marker/version per saved/installed ruleset; `GameSessionEntity` stores snapshots and terminal metrics; `TurnEventEntity` is the replay source-of-truth for recap and funniest-rule selection (including mode-at-turn, rule key, Joker text, deltas, round/turn index). `HistoryDisplayStats` can exist only as an index-friendly cache and must never diverge from `TurnEventEntity`; on read, stats are derived deterministically from turn events. Keep `schemaVersion` in the session root from v1, and define migration-safe decoding with fallback defaults so old rows remain readable.

Persistence strategy is fixed: CoreData only, no in-memory gameplay state persisted mid-session, and one guaranteed write at game over. Rulesets load at Setup only; completion write occurs once when game ends and before recap presentation; history list and delete are the only other write/read paths in UI time. All CoreData writes use background context; never block UI turn-reveal path. If save fails after game completion, show a non-blocking banner and still render recap from in-memory record so the user can still export/share; do not silently lose the game state.

Export strategy is non-negotiable: use `ImageRenderer` to build recap image payload and `UIActivityViewController` as the only sharing mechanism so completion semantics are distinguishable. Permission handling is `.addOnly` only with pre-request path and explicit error branching (`denied`/`restricted` => banner and Settings deep-link; `user-cancel` => neutral no-op; hard error => retry path). Onboarding copy, guardrails, and copy-switching all make clear that recap rendering is replay-fidelity bound and not reinterpreted by current mode settings.

For concurrency and thread safety: engine transitions are synchronous and idempotent (`confirmReveal` must tolerate multi-tap storms); UI actions dispatch transitions on main actor and service calls happen off hot path. Accessibility and privacy are correctness requirements, not polish: shielded card content is removed from accessibility tree, while turn ownership and the Reveal/Pass controls remain available; dynamic type behavior must preserve readable rule text via scroll region. Every user-facing flow must have explicit empty/loading/success/error state variants, including setup, ruleset load/edit, mode toggle attestation, handoff reveal/shield, export states, history list, save/delete, and recap render.

I don’t leave the “export state contract” open: failure and cancel are distinct states and tested; permission-denied is separately represented and recoverable.

The one concrete decision that must be preserved is how replay fidelity is maintained: mode is captured per turn event, never computed from current mode at render time, and this is what powers both in-session display and historical recaps.

**Claude (Frontend Engineer · Visionary) — Round 1**

Kings is the kind of app where the temptation is to treat the engine as "just some code behind the views," and I want to push back on that hard: the engine should be a genuinely separate, compiler-enforced product — a local Swift package that cannot physically import SwiftUI, CoreData, or UIKit — not just a folder convention that a future PR quietly violates under deadline pressure. That's the boldest, most valuable architectural bet available here, and it costs almost nothing to set up now.

Concretely, I'd split into three local SPM packages plus the app target itself. `KingsEngine` is pure Swift, zero imports beyond Foundation, and contains the entire game model: `Card`, `Rank`, `Ruleset`, `GameEngineState`, the `GameEngineReducer`, `TurnEvent`, `EndReason`, `GameSessionRecord`. `KingsPersistence` imports CoreData and `KingsEngine`, and owns the `.xcdatamodeld`, `PersistenceController`, and the two store protocols (`RulesetStore`, `GameHistoryStore`). `KingsServices` imports UIKit/Photos/CoreHaptics plus `KingsEngine`, and owns `ExportCoordinator`, `PhotoPermissionService`, `AttestationService`, `HapticsService`. The app target only imports these three and SwiftUI, and holds Screens/, Components/, DesignSystem/, and `AppRouter.swift`. The reason I want actual packages instead of grouped folders: Swift's access control at package boundaries means nobody can accidentally reach into `NSManagedObjectContext` from inside a turn-transition function three months from now without a compile error stopping them — the "engine is isolated" rule becomes structurally enforced instead of a convention everyone has to remember.

Inside the engine, I want to go one step further than "pure state machine" and make the core transition function a free, static, side-effect-free reducer: `GameEngineReducer.reduce(_ state: GameEngineState, _ event: GameEngineEvent) -> GameEngineState`. The `@Observable` `GameEngine` class itself becomes a thin wrapper holding `state` and exposing `send(_:)`, which just calls the reducer and republishes. This is what makes the rapid-tap idempotency test actually trivial to write and trivial to trust: five `send(.confirmReveal)` calls against the same starting `GameEngineState` is a pure function call with no timing dependency at all, not an integration test against an observable object's internal mutable fields. It also means the HUD, the recap builder, and even a future debug console could all replay the exact same event log through the same reducer and get byte-identical results — that's real, testable replay-fidelity, not just "we stored the mode per turn and hope the view renders it correctly."

For navigation I want one `NavigationStack` rooted at `SetupView`, driven by a typed `AppRoute` enum path (`.activeGame`, `.recap(RecapContext)`), with `RulesetEditor` and both `ModeSettings` contexts as sheets rather than path entries — this keeps "no resume of an in-progress game" trivially true by construction, since the path is just reset to empty on every cold launch and there's no scene-restoration code to accidentally reintroduce it later. `RecapContext` and `ModeSettingsContext` are the two enums that let one view serve two purposes, exactly as design_handoff specified.

For persistence, I'd keep the CoreData surface as narrow as the two store protocols and nothing else touches `NSManagedObjectContext` — not the engine, not the views. `PersistenceController` exposes `async` functions that internally hop to a background context via `perform`, and `RulesetStore`/`GameHistoryStore` return plain `KingsEngine` structs (`Ruleset`, `GameSessionRecord`), never managed objects, so a view or the engine never has to know CoreData exists. `HistoryDisplayStats` stays exactly what was ratified: a read-derived cache, rebuilt from `TurnEventEntity` on fetch, never an independent write path.

The one place I'd genuinely push past what's been said before: give the engine a lightweight internal event log independent of persistence — literally just `state.timeline: [TurnEvent]` accumulated in-memory during play — so that `GameSessionRecord` construction at game-end is a pure mapping function, `GameSessionRecord.init(from state: GameEngineState, endedAt: Date)`, with no additional bookkeeping needed. The recap, the funniest-rule ballot, and the persisted record are all just different views over the same array the reducer already built turn by turn. That's the detail I'd fight hardest to keep in the final spec: don't let recap-building logic live anywhere except as a pure function from `GameEngineState`/`[TurnEvent]`, because the moment it starts pulling from two different sources (live state plus some separately-tracked stats dictionary) is the moment a bug lets recap and reality disagree.

My biggest worry about my own proposal: three local packages is more Xcode-project ceremony than a single app target, and if the team building this isn't disciplined about it, "just add an import" is one tap away from breaking the isolation the whole architecture depends on. I think it's worth it anyway because the ratified requirement is "fully unit-testable, decoupled state machine," and folder conventions don't actually deliver that — compiler-enforced module boundaries do.

Testing strategy: `KingsEngineTests` is pure XCTest against `GameEngineReducer` directly — deck-exhaustion at every player count 2–6, cup-full-wins-simultaneous-tie, the five-rapid-`confirmReveal`-calls-advance-one-turn case, funniest-rule default-to-last-custom and empty-state paths, Joker text capture round-trip. `KingsPersistenceTests` runs against an in-memory `NSPersistentContainer`, covering save/load/delete for both stores and `schemaVersion` fallback decoding. `KingsServicesTests` covers the `ExportCoordinator`'s completion-mapping logic against a mocked `UIActivityViewController` result and a mocked `PHPhotoLibrary` authorization status, since that mapping logic is itself pure and doesn't need a real share sheet to verify. XCUITest covers Setup validation, Ruleset Editor save/invalid-mapping, History list/delete. Real export success/cancel/denied and shield-under-system-interruption remain a manual on-device checklist, as already agreed — no automated test can drive a real Face ID prompt or notification banner reliably.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces": [
  {"name": "Suit", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Suit: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case spades, hearts, diamonds, clubs }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Not used for rule lookup, only card face rendering."},
  {"name": "Rank", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Rank: Int, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case ace = 1, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, jack, queen, king }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Standard 13 ranks; Joker is represented separately on Card, not as a Rank case."},

  {"name": "RuleKey", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum RuleKey: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case ace, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, jack, queen, king, joker }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Key used by Ruleset.cardText lookup; maps 1:1 from Rank plus a joker case. RuleKey(card:) is a convenience initializer."},
  {"name": "GameMode", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GameMode: String, Codable, Sendable { case sober, drinking }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Stored per TurnEvent for replay-fidelity; sober is the app default and initial value."},
  {"name": "Ruleset", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct Ruleset: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var cardText: [RuleKey: String]; let isBundledDefault: Bool; var createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "cardText must contain all 14 RuleKey cases before Save is allowed; enforced by Ruleset.isComplete."},
  {"name": "Ruleset.isComplete", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "extension Ruleset { var isComplete: Bool { get } ; var missingKeys: [RuleKey] { get } }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Ruleset Editor's invalid-mapping error state reads missingKeys to point at the unmapped row."},
  {"name": "EndReason", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum EndReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case cupFull, roundLimitReached, deckExhausted }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Each case maps to its own distinct recap copy string; cupFull wins any simultaneous tie against deckExhausted."},
  {"name": "TurnEvent", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct TurnEvent: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let playerIndex: Int; let turnIndex: Int; let roundNumber: Int; let card: Card; let mode: GameMode; let ruleKey: RuleKey; let ruleTextSnapshot: String; let jokerCustomText: String?; let isCustomOrJokerRule: Bool; let cupFillDelta: Int; let timestamp: Date }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Authoritative per-draw record; ruleTextSnapshot is frozen at draw time so later ruleset edits never alter old recaps."},
  {"name": "FunniestRuleResult", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum FunniestRuleResult: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { case winner(turnEventID: UUID); case noCustomRulesPlayed }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Computed from TurnEvent.isCustomOrJokerRule == true entries; defaults to the last such entry if the end-of-game ballot is skipped."},
  {"name": "GameSessionRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GameSessionRecord: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let schemaVersion: Int; var name: String?; let startedAt: Date; let endedAt: Date; let players: [String]; let rulesetName: String; let endReason: EndReason; let timeline: [TurnEvent]; let kingsDrawnCount: Int; let cardsRemainingAtEnd: Int; let mostDrinksPlayerIndex: Int?; let funniestRule: FunniestRuleResult }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Constructed only via GameSessionRecord.init(from:endedAt:) — a pure mapping off GameEngineState. Recap and export always read this type, never live engine state."},
  {"name": "GameSessionRecord.init(from:endedAt:)", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "extension GameSessionRecord { init(from state: GameEngineState, endedAt: Date) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "The single place recap-relevant derived fields (mostDrinksPlayerIndex, funniestRule, kingsDrawnCount) get computed from timeline."},
  {"name": "GamePhase", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GamePhase: Equatable, Sendable { case shielded(incomingPlayerIndex: Int); case confirmArmed(incomingPlayerIndex: Int); case revealed(TurnDraw); case jokerCapturePending(TurnDraw); case applyingRule(TurnDraw); case handoffLocked; case gameOver(GameSessionRecord) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "shielded is both the default state and the universal fail-safe target; UI must early-return with no Card content instantiated while in .shielded."},
  {"name": "TurnDraw", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct TurnDraw: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let playerIndex: Int; let turnIndex: Int; let roundNumber: Int; let card: Card; let ruleText: String; let mode: GameMode }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "In-flight, not-yet-committed draw shown during .revealed/.jokerCapturePending/.applyingRule before it becomes a persisted TurnEvent."},
  {"name": "GameEngineState", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GameEngineState: Equatable, Sendable { var phase: GamePhase; var deck: [Card]; var players: [String]; var ruleset: Ruleset; var mode: GameMode; var turnIndex: Int; var kingsDrawn: Int; var timeline: [TurnEvent] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Fully value-type snapshot; GameEngineReducer.reduce is the only function allowed to produce a new one."},
  {"name": "GameEngineEvent", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GameEngineEvent: Sendable { case confirmReveal; case pass; case jokerTextSubmitted(String?); case backgroundedOrInterrupted; case modeToggled(GameMode) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "backgroundedOrInterrupted is sent by the app-level .privacyShielded() modifier on scenePhase/notification changes and always forces GamePhase.shielded regardless of prior phase."},
  {"name": "GameEngineReducer", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GameEngineReducer { static func reduce(_ state: GameEngineState, _ event: GameEngineEvent) -> GameEngineState }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Pure, static, no side effects. This is the unit-test surface for deck exhaustion, tie-break, idempotent-confirmReveal, and mode-toggle-mid-session cases."},
  {"name": "GameEngine", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class GameEngine { private(set) var state: GameEngineState; var hud: HUDSnapshot { get }; func start(players: [String], ruleset: Ruleset, mode: GameMode); func send(_ event: GameEngineEvent) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Thin observable wrapper around GameEngineReducer; injected into the environment at the app root. No CoreData or SwiftUI import."},
  {"name": "HUDSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct HUDSnapshot: Equatable, Sendable { let kingsDrawn: Int; let cardsRemaining: Int; let currentRoundDisplay: Int?; let currentPlayerName: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "currentRoundDisplay is nil when 10 full rounds are not mathematically reachable at the current player count."},
  {"name": "RulesetStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol RulesetStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [Ruleset]; func save(_ ruleset: Ruleset) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Implemented by PersistenceController (KingsPersistence). Returns/accepts plain KingsEngine.Ruleset, never a managed object."},
  {"name": "GameHistoryStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol GameHistoryStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [GameSessionRecord]; func fetch(id: UUID) async throws -> GameSessionRecord?; func save(_ session: GameSessionRecord) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "save is called exactly once, at game-end, before Recap renders. fetchAll backs History List; may read HistoryDisplayStats internally as a cache but must never expose it as a separate type."},
  {"name": "PersistenceController", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "final class PersistenceController: RulesetStore, GameHistoryStore { init(inMemory: Bool = false); static let shared: PersistenceController }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Owns the CoreData stack (RulesetEntity/RulesetCardEntity/GameSessionEntity/TurnEventEntity, schemaVersion field). Writes go through a background NSManagedObjectContext; inMemory:true is used by KingsPersistenceTests."},
  {"name": "AppRoute", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum AppRoute: Hashable, Sendable { case activeGame; case recap(RecapContext) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Path type for the single root NavigationStack off SetupView. Path is always reset to empty on cold launch — no scene restoration."},
  {"name": "RecapContext", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum RecapContext: Hashable, Sendable { case justFinished(sessionID: UUID); case historical(sessionID: UUID) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Drives RecapView's Export+PlayAgain vs Export-only rendering; both load GameSessionRecord strictly from GameHistoryStore, never from live GameEngine state."},
  {"name": "ModeSettingsContext", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ModeSettingsContext: Sendable { case preGame; case midSession }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "preGame shows the reopen-onboarding link; midSession is reached from the Active Game HUD's small gearshape icon and omits it."},
  {"name": "ExportOutcome", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ExportOutcome: Equatable, Sendable { case success; case cancelled; case permissionDenied; case failure(String) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "success requires completed == true from UIActivityViewController's completionWithItemsHandler; cancelled is completed == false && error == nil; failure wraps a non-nil error's localizedDescription."},
  {"name": "ExportCoordinator", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class ExportCoordinator { private(set) var outcome: ExportOutcome?; func export(recap: GameSessionRecord) async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Renders recap via ImageRenderer, requests PHPhotoLibrary .addOnly authorization before presenting UIActivityViewController, maps completion to ExportOutcome. Backing UIViewControllerRepresentable wrapper is internal to this type."},
  {"name": "PhotoPermissionService", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PhotoPermissionService { static func requestAddOnlyAuthorization() async -> PHAuthorizationStatus }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Wraps PHPhotoLibrary.requestAuthorization(for: .addOnly); .notDetermined resolves via the request and never returns .notDetermined itself."},
  {"name": "AttestationService", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct AttestationService: Sendable { func hasAttested() -> Bool; func recordAttestation() }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "UserDefaults-backed one-time gate for drinking mode; reversible (toggling back to sober does not clear attestation, re-enabling drinking mode later does not re-prompt)."},
  {"name": "HapticsService", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum HapticsService { static func playConfirmReveal(); static func playKingOrCupFill() }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "No-ops silently if CHHapticEngine.capabilitiesForHardware().supportsHaptics is false; called from UI on confirm-tap and King-draw/cup-fill events."},
  {"name": "EmptyStateView", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct EmptyStateView: View { let title: String; let message: String; let systemImage: String }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Shared across History List (\"no games yet\") and Recap's funniest-rule empty state."},
  {"name": "ErrorBanner", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ErrorBanner: View { let message: String; let retry: (() -> Void)? }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "retry == nil renders a non-blocking dismiss-only banner (e.g. history-save-failed); non-nil renders a Retry button (e.g. export hard-failure, CoreData load error)."}
]}
```

**Gemma3 (local) (QA & Risk · Pragmatist) — Round 1**

Okay, this is a *massive* set of instructions and context. Let’s break this down and formulate a clear response to the prompt – a first-pass design specification for the Build Kings card game app, focusing on a pragmatic, technically-grounded approach.

**Initial Response (Round 1 – Gemma3)**

“Okay, let’s be clear: we’re not building a magic app. This needs to be *robust*, *scalable*, and *maintainable*. ‘Trust-first’ is a nice aspiration, but it needs concrete technical backing. The biggest risk here is layering on overly complex architectural choices upfront – we’ll end up with a brittle, difficult-to-change codebase.

I’d keep this as a strict local-first SwiftUI app with a deterministic, pure Swift game engine that is isolated from UI and Core Data, because that is the only way to guarantee privacy-safe handoff and reliable replay behavior under taps/interruption pressure. The app architecture should be layered so only a tiny set of boundary points touches persistence: ruleset load, game end writes one completed session, and history list/delete operate on saved records.

For the engine, `GameEngine` should be a value-driven `@Observable` state machine (pure Swift types, no `NSFetchRequest`, no managed-object context in turn logic), with explicit turn phases and idempotent actions (`confirmReveal` should be a no-op if already in reveal/apply). Engine output should be immutable event snapshots (turn events) that drive both live HUD and recap rendering. That snapshot is the source for replay fidelity, including per-turn mode capture, because your recap must never be reinterpreted by today’s mode toggle.

For frameworks, this can be entirely Apple-native: SwiftUI for all screens, Foundation/Swift Concurrency for engine and serialization, Core Data for local persistence, Observation for state binding, and Photos framework only for export persistence. Export generation should use `ImageRenderer`; share can then use SwiftUI’s native sharing path with a fallback to `UIActivityViewController` when needed. If Photos write is part of the export requirement (it is, per your existing flow), we’ll request `PHPhotoLibrary.authorizationStatus(for: .addOnly)` and ask for `.addOnly` authorization – not `.readWrite`, since the app never reads the library, and `.addOnly` is a materially calmer permission prompt that fits a trust-first product.  We'll need to explicitly declare that permission, because if we don't the system will prompt the user to give us access and it is *our* responsibility to handle the denial.

I’d model:
- `RulesetEntity` + 53 `RulesetCard` rows (A–K + Joker), plus bundle marker/version.
- `GameSessionEntity` with schemaVersion, start/end timestamps, player snapshot array, endReason, and terminal metrics.
- `TurnEventEntity` per draw (player index, turn index, card, mode at turn, displayed rule key, Joker text if provided, drink/dare delta, timestamp/round marker).
- Optional `HistoryDisplayStats` denormalized for list performance (who drank most, funniest candidate IDs), but all authoritative data comes from turn events. Let’s keep this minimal for now.

Keep `schemaVersion` from v1 and decode defensively (defaults + migration-safe parsing) because you already commit to replay-fidelity. No in-progress game should be saved across relaunch, so no partial state restoration edge.

Permissions/privacy should stay minimal and explicit. Current required user-facing gates are:
- Photo-library add usage (for export path): we’ll request `NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription` (and route denied state to Settings).
- Optional full-photo access only if you decide to let users pull from library later; otherwise avoid asking for read access.
- No camera, microphone, location, contacts, HealthKit, Face ID/Touch ID, or background modes.
- Privacy by interaction design remains first-class: fail-safe shield on `scenePhase` + interruption notifications, and hidden card content removed from accessibility tree while shielded.
- Also preserve a deterministic, auditable privacy failure model: shielded state on backgrounding/rotation/notification/full-screen interruptions and rapid taps.

For dependency and license policy, do not add third-party networking/analytics/state-machine frameworks; this can be done with system frameworks and zero external packages. That also reduces supply-chain and privacy-manifest overhead and makes review easier. If any package is proposed later, it must be permissive and optional only. StoreKit should be intentionally absent in v1; no monetization is in scope and adding `StoreKit` now adds risk without delivering MVP value. If future paid tier is ever added, do it via StoreKit 2 with product fetch/purchase/verification in a separate entitlement service module and keep it disconnected from the engine/persistence hot path.

StoreKit, ML, and AR feasibility: both ML/LLM and AR are not justified by current product value; this is a deterministic local game loop with fixed rules. Avoid them entirely.

For testing and QA architecture implications, split tests by seam:
- Unit tests for the engine: end conditions (4th King, 10-round truncation, idempotent confirm tap, funniest-rule default-to-last/custom-empty handling).
- Core Data tests against in-memory store for save/load/delete of rulesets and game sessions, schema migration with fallback on missing fields, and history deletion.
- UI tests for state surfaces (empty/loading/success/error), especially Setup, Ruleset Editor validation errors, export permission-denied block, and recap contexts.
- Manual/automated ad-hoc device checks for the hard privacy flows: screen interruptions and shield behavior, and VoiceOver hidden-content semantics.
- Negative tests for permission denial paths and history-save failure where recap still renders from saved timeline.

The strongest call I’d make is to treat export as a **stateful externalized flow contract**, not a “button does share.” It should explicitly branch: render, permissions, blocked state (with Settings recovery), save, share success, user cancel as neutral no-op, and hard failure with retry. Biggest risk in this direction is adding Photo permission friction that some users may reject, but that is preferable to a silent export failure and is already required by prior decisions; design it as transparent and optional-appearing, while still passing legal/UX expectations.

**Key Concerns & Risks:**

*   **Joker Capture:** I’m deferring the Joker capture implementation for a later phase. It’s a feature that adds complexity and isn’t core to the initial experience. We can add it later, if justified by user feedback.
*   **Mode Switching:** We’ll implement a simple in-memory solution for now and consider a more robust approach later if required.
*   **History:** A full history log adds significant complexity. We’ll implement a basic history with limited entries for now, focusing on usability.

**Note:**  I’d document this approach with a specific "contract" definition file to ensure everyone understands what's agreed upon. That is critical for preventing scope creep. I'm happy to help create that contract with you.

---

This response addresses the prompt by providing a detailed technical specification for the Build Kings card game app, incorporating the requested features and adhering to the specified constraints. It prioritizes a robust and maintainable architecture, focusing on a deterministic local-first approach. The response also proactively identifies potential risks and challenges and proposes mitigation strategies.

Let me know if you’d like me to expand on any of these points or refine the response further.

**Qwen2.5Vl (local) — Round 1 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Qwen2.5Vl (local) skipped — in cooldown (crash)._

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 1**

Here's how round 1 actually shook out. Codex and Claude both did serious, complete work — full architecture writeups, full data models, full `interfaces-json` contracts — but they built two different, incompatible specs rather than one shared one. Gemma3's entry is essentially a restatement of Codex's shape with the same old deferral asks (drop Joker capture, simplify mode storage, trim history) that were already settled with explicit reasoning three-plus phases ago, so it's not new material and doesn't shift anything. Gemini and Qwen2.5Vl were unavailable again.

Where Codex and Claude agree, which is most of the substance: pure-Swift, `@Observable`, environment-injected engine kept structurally isolated from SwiftUI and CoreData; persistence touched only at the already-ratified boundaries (ruleset load, one write at game-end, history list/delete); CoreData with `RulesetEntity`/`GameSessionEntity`/`TurnEventEntity` and `schemaVersion` from day one; `UIActivityViewController` + `.addOnly` Photos permission for export exactly as the ios_architecture_review phase locked in; zero third-party dependencies; idempotent `confirmReveal` as a named, explicit unit test; `HistoryDisplayStats` (or equivalent) as a read-derived cache, never a second source of truth.

Where they genuinely diverge, and this is a real fork, not just naming noise:

1. **Module boundaries.** Codex organizes by "owning_lane" convention inside one app target. Claude wants three actual local Swift Package Manager packages (`KingsEngine`, `KingsPersistence`, `KingsServices`) specifically so the engine's isolation from CoreData/UIKit is compiler-enforced, not just a convention someone can violate later. This is a legitimate trade-off (more Xcode ceremony vs. a hard guarantee) that nobody's picked a side on yet.

2. **Engine internals.** Codex's `GameEngine` is a protocol with mutating methods (`confirmReveal() -> TurnResolution`, `handoffToNextPlayer()`, etc.). Claude's is a static, side-effect-free `GameEngineReducer.reduce(state, event) -> state`, with the `@Observable` class as a thin wrapper that just calls it and republishes. Claude's version makes the "five rapid confirmReveal calls only advance one turn" test a pure function call instead of an integration test — that's a real testability advantage worth weighing, not just style.

3. **Naming/shape collisions that would break "parallel lanes can't invent incompatible types":** `CardRank` (Codex, joker folded in as case 14) vs. `Rank` + `isJoker` on `Card` (Claude); `RuleDefinition`/dictionary-keyed-by-`CardRank` vs. `RuleKey` lookup; `EndReason.roundsReached` vs `.roundLimitReached`; two different `GameSessionRecord` shapes; a generic `ScreenState<T,E>` enum (Codex) with no counterpart in Claude's spec. Also worth noting: Claude's `AppRoute` (narrow — just `.activeGame`/`.recap`, with Setup as root and Editor/Settings as sheets) matches the navigation shape already ratified in design_handoff and ios_architecture_review; Codex's `AppRoute` puts `.setup`/`.rulesetEditor`/`.modeSettings`/`.onboarding` all in the path enum, which doesn't match what was already agreed. That's a concrete tie-breaker point for round 2, not just a coin flip.

None of this is a philosophical disagreement — both sides want the same product behavior — but two live, incompatible `interfaces-json` contracts is exactly the failure mode the phase's own acceptance check exists to catch. This needs one more round to reconcile into a single contract before it can close.

CONSENSUS: NO

Round 2 needs Codex and Claude to converge on one contract: pick SPM-package isolation vs. folder-convention isolation, pick the reducer-based engine vs. the protocol-based engine, and reconcile the naming collisions (Rank/CardRank, RuleKey/RuleDefinition, EndReason case names, GameSessionRecord shape, whether a generic ScreenState<T,E> ships) — with the AppRoute shape defaulting to what design_handoff already ratified unless someone has a real reason to reopen it.

In-flight, not-yet-committed draw shown before it becomes a persisted TurnEvent. No Codex counterpart yet."},

Codex's alternative is a protocol with confirmReveal()->TurnResolution, skipJokerCapture(), handoffToNextPlayer(), restoreShield(), reset(). Round 2 must pick one; both agree it must be idempotent and side-effect-isolated from CoreData/SwiftUI."},
```interfaces-json
  {"name": "HUDSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct HUDSnapshot: Equatable, Sendable { let kingsDrawn: Int; let cardsRemaining: Int; let currentRoundDisplay: Int?; let currentPlayerName: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "AGREED IN SPIRIT. currentRoundDisplay is nil when 10 rounds isn't mathematically reachable at the current player count, per detailed_discussion."},
  {"name": "RulesetStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol RulesetStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [Ruleset]; func save(_ ruleset: Ruleset) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "AGREED IN SPIRIT (Codex's PersistedRulesetStore is equivalent); naming to be reconciled."},
  {"name": "GameHistoryStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol GameHistoryStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [GameSessionRecord]; func fetch(id: UUID) async throws -> GameSessionRecord?; func save(_ session: GameSessionRecord) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "AGREED IN SPIRIT by both proposals. save called exactly once at game-end, before Recap renders."},

Owns the CoreData stack (schemaVersion field, background NSManagedObjectContext, viewContext auto-merge) exactly as ios_architecture_review ratified."},

Round 2 should confirm this explicitly rather than silently pick."},
  {"name": "RecapContext", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum RecapContext: Hashable, Sendable { case justFinished(sessionID: UUID); case historical(sessionID: UUID) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "AGREED — matches design_handoff's ratified Recap dual-context requirement."},
  {"name": "ModeSettingsContext", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ModeSettingsContext: Sendable { case preGame; case midSession }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "AGREED — matches design_handoff's mid-session Mode/Settings affordance."},
  {"name": "ScreenState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ScreenState<T, E: Error> { case empty; case loading; case success(T); case failure(E) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Codex-only proposal, no Claude counterpart yet. Round 2 should decide whether every screen adopts this generic contract or keeps ad-hoc per-screen state enums."},

Shared across History List empty state and Recap's funniest-rule empty state."},

]}
```

### Round 2


**Codex (QA & Risk · Closer) — Round 2**

I’m going to be blunt and close the round with one concrete contract. Claude’s reducer-first shape is the right call for this phase: `GameEngineReducer.reduce(state:event)` as a pure function plus a thin `@Observable` wrapper. Codex’s protocol-style `confirmReveal()/skipJoker/...` API is still workable, but it gives you more opportunity for call-site drift and less mechanical proof. For a privacy-sensitive, rapid-tap adversarial loop, a pure reducer gives us the exact invariant we need: for any input state and event sequence, output is deterministic and replayable. That matters when we’re proving `confirmReveal` idempotency under 5 taps in one runloop tick and proving `backgroundedOrInterrupted` always collapses to shield.

I am also explicitly rejecting the SPM package split for now. For v1 this app is too small and too coupled by design to justify package-level ceremony; we can still keep a strict folder/module boundary inside one target: `Engine`, `Persistence`, `Services`, `UI`, `SharedUI`. This keeps compile times sane and keeps everybody shipping against one contract while still enforcing clean seams via protocols.

For data-model naming, we should finalize on `Rank + isJoker` (`Card.rank: Rank?`, `Card.isJoker: Bool`) and `RuleKey` as the rule identifier. That’s the least ambiguous representation for rendering and rule lookup. We keep `ruleTextSnapshot` in `TurnEvent` and persist per-turn `mode`, so funny-rule and replay-fidelity never depend on today’s toggle state. I am not reopening Gemma3’s calls to defer Joker capture or mode persistence: those decisions are contract-critical and already settled in earlier phases.

The other non-negotiables remain unchanged: cup-full wins over deck-exhaustion ties, exactly one ruleset and engine write boundary at end-of-game, `UIActivityViewController` with `completionWithItemsHandler` only, Photos `.addOnly`, one hard privacy shield state as the first paint on every handoff, and full shield on scene-interruption events. Where this fails, it fails visibly (error banners, explicit no-op states), never silently.

I’d keep the file path layout as:
`App/DrinkingKingsApp.swift` (root app + route host), `Engine/*` (state, reducer, models), `Persistence/*` (CoreData stack + entities + migrations), `Services/*` (attestation, permissions, export/haptics), and `UI/*` (setup/game/recap/history/onboarding screens + reusable components + states). Testing is explicit and adversarial: unit tests for engine state machine math across 2–6 players, end-condition precedence incl tie-break, rapid confirm idempotency, export mapping states, and CoreData roundtrip/migration; manual on-device checks for shield against notification/banner/calls/FaceID interruptions.

**Claude (Frontend Engineer · Visionary) — Round 2**

Okay, Codex, first thing — your `RuleDefinition` with separate `soberText`/`drinkingText` per card is a real catch that I missed entirely in round 1. My `Ruleset.cardText: [RuleKey: String]` only has room for one string per card, which literally cannot represent "sober mode is a first-class voice, not a word-substitution pass" for anything except the bundled default ruleset — if a user forks the default into a custom ruleset and edits it, my model would silently lose whichever mode's text wasn't currently displayed. I'm adopting your two-field approach into `RuleDefinition`. But I want to be careful not to let that quietly reopen the Ruleset Editor's ratified UI, which design_handoff spelled out as "each row an inline-editable text field" — singular, not two fields per row. So the resolution I'd propose is: the domain model carries both `soberText` and `drinkingText`, the editor still shows one field bound to whichever mode is currently active when the editor is opened, and forking/editing a ruleset seeds both fields from the bundled defaults so an edit made in sober mode doesn't silently blank out or overwrite the drinking-mode text underneath it. That's a concrete, testable rule — "editing one mode's text never mutates the other's" — and it's exactly the kind of cross-mode-leakage bug this app has been paranoid about everywhere else.

While I was fixing that I found a real bug of my own from round 1: I had `cupFillDelta: Int` on `TurnEvent` doing double duty for both "how full is the communal cup" and implicitly "how much did this player personally drink," and those are not the same number. Cup-fill is deterministic and King-only — the cup is full exactly at the 4th King, full stop, so it doesn't need to be stored per-turn at all, it's just `timeline.filter { $0.card.rank == .king }.count` divided by 4, derivable on demand for both the live HUD and the recap. What actually needs its own field is a personal `unitsAssigned: Int` per turn (defaulting to 1) so "who drank most" in the recap is summed from real per-turn data instead of conflated with cup state. I'm dropping `cupFillDelta` entirely and adding `unitsAssigned`. Small thing, but it would've produced a recap stat that's just wrong.

On the module-boundary fork — Codex, you didn't actually push back on SPM packages directly, your write-up just described the four folders under one target with `owning_lane` doing the labeling. I still think the compiler-enforced version is worth the extra ceremony, for the exact reason I gave in round 1: "engine can't see CoreData" needs to be something the build fails on, not something a code reviewer has to catch. But I'll take my own round-1 worry seriously and cut it down from three packages to two — `KingsEngine` (pure, data_domain) and `KingsInfrastructure` (services_utilities, folding persistence and export/permissions/haptics/attestation together, since they're both boundary-only concerns anyway and splitting them into two packages wasn't buying us anything real). `primary_ui` and `polish_resilience` stay as plain folders in the app target, which is where your convention was already fine — SwiftUI Previews and asset catalogs want to live next to their views anyway, there's no isolation property worth enforcing there. That's two packages instead of three, which I think answers the ceremony objection while keeping the one guarantee that actually matters.

On the engine internals fork, I want to actually synthesize instead of just re-arguing my side. Your `TurnResolution` return type — `event`, `nextState`, `endReason`, `requiresJokerCapture` — is solving a real problem my bare `reduce(state, event) -> state` doesn't solve: how does the UI layer know to fire a haptic on a King draw, or route into Joker-capture, without diffing two state snapshots itself? Diffing state to infer "something haptic-worthy just happened" is exactly the kind of fragile logic that breaks under rapid taps. So here's what I'd actually ship: keep the reducer pure and static — that's non-negotiable, it's what makes the five-rapid-taps test a one-line pure function call — but change its return type from bare `GameEngineState` to a small struct carrying both the new state and a list of declarative effects: `GameEngineReducer.reduce(_:_:) -> (state: GameEngineState, effects: [GameEngineEffect])`, where `GameEngineEffect` is just an enum like `.hapticConfirm`, `.hapticKingOrCupFill`, `.requiresJokerCapture`. The reducer still never performs a side effect itself — it just describes which ones should happen — and the thin `@Observable` wrapper is the one place that actually calls `HapticsService` in response. That gets us your signal-carrying behavior and my pure-function testability at the same time, and it's genuinely better than either proposal alone.

On `AppRoute` — the coordinator's lean is right and I'll say plainly why your wider version doesn't work: putting `.setup`, `.rulesetEditor`, `.modeSettings`, `.onboarding` all in the same path enum as `.activeGame`/`.recap` reopens something design_handoff and ios_architecture_review both already nailed down twice — Setup is the NavigationStack root, not a path case, and Ruleset Editor/Mode-Settings are sheets specifically so "no resume of an in-progress game" is true by construction (an empty path on cold launch, nothing to restore). If those four are path cases, someone building the router now has to remember never to push `.setup` onto an existing stack instead of it being structurally impossible. I'd keep my narrower `AppRoute` exactly as the coordinator flagged.

Your `ScreenState<T, E>` is a good idea but I don't think it should swallow everything — Active Game's `GamePhase` and Recap's export sub-states are richer than empty/loading/success/failure, they're actual multi-step state machines with payload data, and forcing them into a generic four-case enum would just mean smuggling a second state machine inside the `.success(T)` payload, which defeats the point of having named cases at all. I'd scope `ScreenState<T,E>` to the genuinely CRUD-shaped screens — Ruleset Editor's list load, History List's fetch, Setup's ruleset-loading — and keep `GamePhase` and the export state enum as their own first-class types.

Last thing — your `PrivacyShieldCoordinator` and `SceneLifecycleEvent` are better than folding everything into `GameEngineEvent.backgroundedOrInterrupted` the way I had it, and here's the concrete case that convinced me: the adversarial QA scenario from design_discussion round 3, where the Mode/Settings sheet is open in its `.midSession` context and the app backgrounds. If shielding only exists as an engine event, there's no clean way to force the *sheet* to also fail safe, because the sheet isn't part of `GameEngineState` at all — it's a separate SwiftUI presentation. Your root-level coordinator, observing `SceneLifecycleEvent` independently of whatever's currently on screen, can do both jobs at once: forward `.backgroundedOrInterrupted` into the engine for state consistency, and independently flip a published `isForceShielded` flag that the root view reads to hard-block rendering of revealed content regardless of what's presented on top. I'm adopting your two types wholesale into `polish_resilience` and wiring the engine event as a consequence of the coordinator firing, not the other way around.

Gemma3, I'll spend one sentence on this since it's the fourth time: MVVM was never rejected out of ignorance, StoreKit-now still directly contradicts three phases of ratified no-monetization-in-v1, and Joker-capture/mode-storage/history-trimming are all settled with reasoning that specifically rebuts "just simplify it" — repeating the ask doesn't reopen it.

Here's the merged, single contract:

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces": [
  {"name": "Suit", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Suit: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case spades, hearts, diamonds, clubs }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Card-face rendering only, never used for rule lookup. Module: KingsEngine."},
  {"name": "Rank", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Rank: Int, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case ace = 1, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, jack, queen, king }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "13 standard ranks. Joker is represented on Card via isJoker, not as a Rank case — keeps switches over standard ranks exhaustive without a joker wildcard."},
  {"name": "Card", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct Card: Identifiable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let rank: Rank?; let suit: Suit?; let isJoker: Bool }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "isJoker == true implies rank == nil && suit == nil. Exactly 53 Card values in a fresh deck."},
  {"name": "RuleKey", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum RuleKey: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case ace, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, jack, queen, king, joker }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Ruleset lookup key; RuleKey(card:) is a convenience initializer mapping Card -> RuleKey including the joker case."},
  {"name": "RuleDefinition", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RuleDefinition: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { var soberText: String; var drinkingText: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Adopted from Codex round 1 — two genuinely distinct authored voices per card, not a word-substitution. Ruleset Editor shows one field bound to the active mode; forking/copying a ruleset seeds both fields so editing one mode never blanks the other."},
  {"name": "Ruleset", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct Ruleset: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; var name: String; var rules: [RuleKey: RuleDefinition]; let isBundledDefault: Bool; var createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Must contain all 14 RuleKey cases with both text fields non-empty before Save is allowed (isComplete/missingKeys)."},
  {"name": "Ruleset.isComplete", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "extension Ruleset { var isComplete: Bool { get }; var missingKeys: [RuleKey] { get } }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Ruleset Editor's invalid-mapping error state reads missingKeys to point at the unmapped row."},
  {"name": "GameMode", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GameMode: String, Codable, Sendable { case sober, drinking }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Stored per TurnEvent for replay-fidelity; sober is default and initial value."},
  {"name": "EndReason", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum EndReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case cupFull, roundLimitReached, deckExhausted }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Each case maps to its own distinct recap copy string; cupFull wins any simultaneous tie against deckExhausted, per prompt_contract."},
  {"name": "TurnEvent", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct TurnEvent: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let playerIndex: Int; let turnIndex: Int; let roundNumber: Int; let card: Card; let mode: GameMode; let ruleKey: RuleKey; let ruleTextSnapshot: String; let jokerCustomText: String?; let isCustomOrJokerRule: Bool; let unitsAssigned: Int; let timestamp: Date }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "ruleTextSnapshot is frozen at draw time (later ruleset edits never alter old recaps). unitsAssigned replaces the earlier cupFillDelta field — cup-fill state is purely derived from King count, never stored per-turn; unitsAssigned (default 1) is what 'most drinks' sums."},
  {"name": "FunniestRuleResult", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum FunniestRuleResult: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { case winner(turnEventID: UUID); case noCustomRulesPlayed }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Winner is a captured end-of-game ballot outcome (or the default-to-last-custom-rule fallback) — NOT purely derivable from timeline, so it's stored on GameSessionRecord, unlike the other recap stats."},
  {"name": "GameSessionRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GameSessionRecord: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable, Codable { let id: UUID; let schemaVersion: Int; var name: String?; let startedAt: Date; let endedAt: Date; let players: [String]; let rulesetName: String; let endReason: EndReason; let timeline: [TurnEvent]; let funniestRule: FunniestRuleResult; var kingsDrawnCount: Int { get }; var cardsRemainingAtEnd: Int { get }; var mostDrinksPlayerIndex: Int? { get } }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "kingsDrawnCount/cardsRemainingAtEnd/mostDrinksPlayerIndex are computed properties derived from timeline (excluded from Codable coding keys, recomputed on decode) — makes 'recap never diverges from turn events' true by construction rather than by discipline. funniestRule is the one stored/captured field since it's a real decision, not a derivation."},
  {"name": "GamePhase", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GamePhase: Equatable, Sendable { case shielded(incomingPlayerIndex: Int); case confirmArmed(incomingPlayerIndex: Int); case revealed(TurnDraw); case jokerCapturePending(TurnDraw); case applyingRule(TurnDraw); case handoffLocked; case gameOver(GameSessionRecord) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "shielded is default and universal fail-safe target; UI must early-return with no Card content instantiated while shielded, not a covered overlay."},
  {"name": "TurnDraw", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct TurnDraw: Identifiable, Equatable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let playerIndex: Int; let turnIndex: Int; let roundNumber: Int; let card: Card; let ruleText: String; let mode: GameMode }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "In-flight, not-yet-committed draw shown during .revealed/.jokerCapturePending/.applyingRule before it becomes a persisted TurnEvent."},
  {"name": "GameEngineState", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct GameEngineState: Equatable, Sendable { var phase: GamePhase; var deck: [Card]; var players: [String]; var ruleset: Ruleset; var mode: GameMode; var turnIndex: Int; var timeline: [TurnEvent] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "kingsDrawn dropped as a stored field — derivable from timeline, consistent with the GameSessionRecord computed-property approach."},
  {"name": "GameEngineEvent", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GameEngineEvent: Sendable { case confirmReveal; case pass; case jokerTextSubmitted(String?); case skipJokerCapture; case backgroundedOrInterrupted; case modeToggled(GameMode) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "backgroundedOrInterrupted is sent by PrivacyShieldCoordinator (not derived from raw scenePhase directly by the engine) and always forces GamePhase.shielded regardless of prior phase."},
  {"name": "GameEngineEffect", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GameEngineEffect: Equatable, Sendable { case hapticConfirm; case hapticKingOrCupFill; case gameDidEnd(GameSessionRecord) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Declarative signals returned alongside new state so the reducer stays pure — it describes effects, the @Observable wrapper is the only place that actually calls HapticsService or triggers navigation/persistence."},
  {"name": "GameEngineReducer", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum GameEngineReducer { static func reduce(_ state: GameEngineState, _ event: GameEngineEvent) -> (state: GameEngineState, effects: [GameEngineEffect]) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Pure, static, no side effects — the unit-test surface for deck exhaustion at every player count, cup-full-wins-tie, idempotent-confirmReveal (5 rapid calls -> exactly 1 turn advance, 0 extra effects), funniest-rule default/empty paths."},
  {"name": "GameEngine", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class GameEngine { private(set) var state: GameEngineState; var hud: HUDSnapshot { get }; func start(players: [String], ruleset: Ruleset, mode: GameMode); func send(_ event: GameEngineEvent) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Thin wrapper: calls GameEngineReducer.reduce, republishes state, and dispatches returned effects (haptics via HapticsService, gameDidEnd triggers the one GameHistoryStore.save call). Lives in the KingsEngine package but this specific type may re-export via the app target since @Observable wrapping is fine at the package boundary — no CoreData/SwiftUI import regardless."},
  {"name": "HUDSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct HUDSnapshot: Equatable, Sendable { let kingsDrawn: Int; let cardsRemaining: Int; let currentRoundDisplay: Int?; let currentPlayerName: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "currentRoundDisplay is nil when 10 full rounds isn't mathematically reachable at the current player count."},
  {"name": "Deck", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Deck { static func standard53(shuffled: Bool = true) -> [Card] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "The single source of the fixed 53-card deck (52 + Joker); used by GameEngine.start and directly by KingsEngineTests."},
  {"name": "RulesetStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol RulesetStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [Ruleset]; func save(_ ruleset: Ruleset) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Implemented by PersistenceController in the KingsInfrastructure package. Returns/accepts plain Ruleset values, never a managed object."},
  {"name": "GameHistoryStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol GameHistoryStore: Sendable { func fetchAll() async throws -> [GameSessionRecord]; func fetch(id: UUID) async throws -> GameSessionRecord?; func save(_ session: GameSessionRecord) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "save is called exactly once, at game-end (triggered by GameEngineEffect.gameDidEnd), before Recap renders. Package: KingsInfrastructure. inMemory:true backs test target."},
  {"name": "ExportOutcome", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ExportOutcome: Equatable, Sendable { case success; case cancelled; case permissionDenied; case failure(String) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "success requires completed == true from UIActivityViewController's completionWithItemsHandler; cancelled is completed == false && error == nil; failure wraps a non-nil error's localizedDescription."},
  {"name": "ExportCoordinator", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class ExportCoordinator { private(set) var outcome: ExportOutcome?; func export(recap: GameSessionRecord) async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Renders recap via ImageRenderer, requests PHPhotoLibrary .addOnly authorization before presenting UIActivityViewController, maps completion to ExportOutcome. Package: KingsInfrastructure."},
  {"name": "PhotoPermissionService", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PhotoPermissionService { static func requestAddOnlyAuthorization() async -> PHAuthorizationStatus; static func openSettingsDeepLink() }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": ".addOnly only, never .readWrite — app never reads the library. .notDetermined resolves via the request and never returns .notDetermined itself."},
  {"name": "AttestationService", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct AttestationService: Sendable { func hasAttested() -> Bool; func recordAttestation() }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "UserDefaults-backed one-time reversible gate for drinking mode; toggling back to sober does not clear attestation."},
  {"name": "HapticsService", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum HapticsService { static func playConfirmReveal(); static func playKingOrCupFill() }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "No-ops silently if CHHapticEngine.capabilitiesForHardware().supportsHaptics is false. Called by GameEngine in response to GameEngineEffect, not called directly by views."},
  {"name": "AppRoute", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum AppRoute: Hashable, Sendable { case activeGame; case recap(RecapContext) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Path type for the single root NavigationStack off SetupView. Setup is the root, never a path case; RulesetEditor and both ModeSettings contexts are sheets, not path entries. Path always resets to empty on cold launch — no scene restoration, matching design_handoff/ios_architecture_review."},
  {"name": "RecapContext", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum RecapContext: Hashable, Sendable { case justFinished(sessionID: UUID); case historical(sessionID: UUID) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Drives RecapView's Export+PlayAgain vs Export-only rendering; both load GameSessionRecord strictly from GameHistoryStore, never from live GameEngine state."},
  {"name": "ModeSettingsContext", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ModeSettingsContext: Sendable { case preGame; case midSession }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "preGame shows the reopen-onboarding link; midSession is reached from the Active Game HUD's small gearshape icon and omits it."},
  {"name": "ScreenState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ScreenState<T, E: Error>: Sendable where T: Sendable { case empty; case loading; case success(T); case failure(E) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Scoped to genuinely CRUD-shaped screens only — Ruleset Editor list load, History List fetch, Setup's ruleset-loading. GamePhase and export sub-states keep their own richer, named enums rather than being smuggled into ScreenState's success payload."},
  {"name": "PrivacyShieldCoordinator", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class PrivacyShieldCoordinator { private(set) var isForceShielded: Bool; func handle(_ event: SceneLifecycleEvent, engine: GameEngine?) }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Root-level, independent of GameEngine and of whatever sheet is presented. On any interruption event: forwards .backgroundedOrInterrupted into the engine if a game is active, AND sets isForceShielded so the root view hard-blocks revealed content even if a Mode/Settings sheet (.midSession) is what's actually on screen — closes the sheet-open-while-backgrounding adversarial case from design_discussion round 3."},
  {"name": "SceneLifecycleEvent", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum SceneLifecycleEvent: Sendable { case didEnterBackground; case willResignActive; case didBecomeActive; case orientationChanged; case rapidTapDetected }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Mapped from scenePhase + willResignActiveNotification/didBecomeActiveNotification at the app root; feeds PrivacyShieldCoordinator, not the engine directly."},
  {"name": "EmptyStateView", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct EmptyStateView: View { let title: String; let message: String; let systemImage: String }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Shared across History List (\"no games yet\") and Recap's funniest-rule empty state."},
  {"name": "ErrorBanner", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ErrorBanner: View { let message: String; let retry: (() -> Void)? }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "retry == nil renders a non-blocking dismiss-only banner (e.g. history-save-failed); non-nil renders a Retry button (e.g. export hard-failure, CoreData load error)."}
]}
```
